spec.reasoning: The previous attempts failed because title_row_insert shifted the data rows, breaking the explicit C2:C6 range reference in the instruction. Since the instruction contains absolute row references (C:2-6) and the risk flags include 'instruction_references_absolute_rows' and 'task_references_specific_cell_addresses', title_row_insert is too dangerous. Instead, I use: (1) column renames — 'Product ID' → 'Item Code', 'Current Location(s)' → 'Bin Location(s)', '# of Bins' → '# of Positions' — these are semantically equivalent and none of these header names appear as general English words in the instruction body; (2) sheet rename 'SKUs as Bins' → 'Product Bin Mapping'; (3) a distractor column 'Warehouse Zone' added after the last column, which is plausibly related but clearly different from existing columns. No title row insert is used, so the C2:C6 answer position and formatting range remain valid. No layout shift occurs.
